SEARCH
Package com.krishcpatel.realm.economy.manager

Class TransactionManager

java.lang.Object
com.krishcpatel.realm.economy.manager.TransactionManager
public class TransactionManager extends Object
Central gateway for all money movement in the server economy.

All balance mutations (player payments, shop purchases, job rewards, upkeep fees, admin adjustments) must go through this class to ensure:

  • Atomic balance changes
  • Append-only ledger recording
  • Consistent monitoring hooks via events

  • Constructor Details

    • TransactionManager

      public TransactionManager(Core core, DatabaseManager db, EconomyRepository economy, LedgerRepository ledger)
      Creates a transaction manager.
      Parameters:
      core - plugin instance used for publishing events and scheduling work
      db - database manager
      economy - repository for account balances
      ledger - repository for ledger persistence

  • Method Details

    • transfer

      public TransactionResult transfer(String fromUuid, String toUuid, long amount, MoneySource source, String reference, String reason, String actor) throws SQLException
      Transfers currency from one player account to another.

      This operation is atomic and will fail if the sender does not have enough funds. A successful transfer is recorded in the ledger and may publish a ledger event.

      Parameters:
      fromUuid - sender UUID (string form)
      toUuid - receiver UUID (string form)
      amount - positive amount to transfer
      source - subsystem source category
      reference - optional external reference id
      reason - optional human-readable reason
      actor - initiator identifier (player UUID, CONSOLE, SYSTEM)
      Returns:
      transaction result including success and ledger id
      Throws:
      SQLException - if database access fails

    • mint

      public TransactionResult mint(String toUuid, long amount, MoneySource source, String reference, String reason, String actor) throws SQLException
      Mints currency into a player's account (creates money).

      Used for job rewards, admin gives, or controlled system rewards. A successful mint is recorded in the ledger and may publish a ledger event.

      Parameters:
      toUuid - receiver UUID (string form)
      amount - positive amount to add
      source - subsystem source category
      reference - optional external reference id
      reason - optional human-readable reason
      actor - initiator identifier (player UUID, CONSOLE, SYSTEM)
      Returns:
      transaction result including success and ledger id
      Throws:
      SQLException - if database access fails

    • burn

      public TransactionResult burn(String fromUuid, long amount, MoneySource source, String reference, String reason, String actor) throws SQLException
      Burns currency from a player's account (removes money).

      Used for upkeep fees, admin takes, or controlled sinks. The burn fails if the account has insufficient funds.

      Parameters:
      fromUuid - sender UUID (string form)
      amount - positive amount to remove
      source - subsystem source category
      reference - optional external reference id
      reason - optional human-readable reason
      actor - initiator identifier (player UUID, CONSOLE, SYSTEM)
      Returns:
      transaction result including success and ledger id
      Throws:
      SQLException - if database access fails

    • setBalance

      public TransactionResult setBalance(String targetUuid, long newBalance, MoneySource source, String reference, String reason, String actor) throws SQLException
      Sets a player's balance to an exact value.

      This is primarily intended for admin corrections. Implementations typically clamp the value to a minimum of 0 and record an entry in the ledger.

      Parameters:
      targetUuid - player UUID (string form)
      newBalance - new balance value
      source - subsystem source category
      reference - optional external reference id
      reason - optional human-readable reason
      actor - initiator identifier (player UUID, CONSOLE, SYSTEM)
      Returns:
      transaction result including success and ledger id
      Throws:
      SQLException - if database access fails
